parsed_review.summary: CP00-710 is a descriptor-only closeout pack extending RP23 External Integrations II (finance-integrations) across 150 units RP23.P06.M00.S07-RP23.P06.M07.S06. Independent read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms the pack-level claims are supported and not over-stated. Registry CP710 binding matches the baseline (Risk C, 150 units, first/last unit IDs, next CP00-711 / RP23.P06.M07.S07); requirements deliverable counts (implementation 56, security_audit 35, ui 28, claude_review 7, test 24) and micro-title row counts match the prompt distribution; service.js/validators.js generate and validate descriptors consistently; program contract is RP23 over upstream RP22; CP709->CP710 ranges are contiguous. model.test.js covers CP710 binding/coverage and a runtime-closed descriptor assertion set (claude_is_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required=true, runtime_opening_pack_id=CP00-711, hermes emits_runtime_receipt=false, claude read_only=true, handoff continuity) plus latest-projection checks. command-evidence.json reports all 25 validation commands at exit 0 (node --check, closeout/rp23 validators, node --test, npm test, npm run validate/spec/weighted/fullplan/goal:closeout/rp00:control-plane, git diff --check, build); these are reported by the pack and were not re-run here since Bash is unavailable to this read-only reviewer. No-write/no-real-data boundaries are enforced via NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION, ROW_EXTRAS, and validateDescriptor (descriptor-only, no runtime, no sensitive payloads/credentials, no cross-tenant, no permission-decision detail, no audit body, no Hermes runtime receipt); no secrets or real financial data appear in source, and the package has no network/fs/child_process surface and no dependencies. CP178+ hardening holds: validation-report prohibits summary-only review (Risk C + security/audit + review-evidence) and records source_inspection_mode read_tools_used; readiness auth-preflight passed (exit 0, 1446-byte valid JSON, no auth failure / 0-byte stdout / malformed JSON); valid_review_receipts and invalid_review_attempts are both empty so no malformed/fenced/tool-shaped output is being counted as valid evidence. Claude remains a read-only, non-final reviewer and Hermes is used only for deterministic descriptor review-packet/receipt handling, not product runtime. The manifest correctly stays status in_progress, production_ready false, runtime_ready false, pending_review. Closeout eligibility is supported; flipping production_ready and advancing to CP00-711 remains a control-plane action after this single valid receipt, with enterprise-trust/runtime-opening reserved for human final approval outside this receipt.
